title: Second unexploded shell found at illegal French rave, says interior minister
subhead: On Saturday evening, bomb disposal experts intervened after a first shell was discovered close to the party site near Bourges, where tens of thousands from France and neighboring countries gathered this weekend.

subhead: From Douarnenez to Rennes, Le Monde toured Brittany's bakeries and pastry shops offering the most tempting takes on this beloved cake enjoyed around the globe.

title: 'Taiwan has become essential for the world's future,' says François Wu, deputy foreign minister
subhead: Two weeks before US President Donald Trump is due to visit Beijing, on May 14 and 15, Taiwanese Deputy Foreign Minister François Wu Chih-chung emphasized Taiwan's increasingly strategic position and the 'useful' global role it can play.

In a small French village, a father is charged with sexual abuse of 34 victims aged 3 to 9
subhead: A tree, a rope, a letter. After surviving a suicide attempt, a man revealed he had committed numerous crimes against children. After a year-long and controversial investigation, he was charged, on April 10, with rape and sexual assault of minors and recording child pornography.
byline: Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Lorraine de Foucher

title: French authors face a reckoning
subhead: Nearly 200 writers have expressed their desire to leave the Grasset publishing house since the dismissal of its chief, Olivier Nora. This mass departure has highlighted their dependence on the publisher, especially regarding rights.

title: How Paris became the capital of master sushi chefs
subhead: In recent years, a small constellation of Michelin-starred 'sushiya' has appeared in the heart of Paris. In these temples of Japanese tradition, renowned sushi chefs have drawn inspiration from French cuisine and local French products to create remarkable and original flavors.
